name: Email Sent to Potentially Hostile Host
notes: This rule triggers when an email is sent to a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host
when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Send Success
TD_list: APPLY Email Sent to Potentially Hostile Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Send Success
RED_list: Event name: Email Sent to Potentially Hostile Host
Event description: Detects when an email is sent to a host that is known for hostile activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Recipients (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
